steps.summary:
file	README.md
dir	lib
file	mix.exs
dir	test

# Budget-Constrained Build Order

Dependency DAG:

- BuildOrder.Parser is foundation A.
- BuildOrder.Rules is foundation B.
- BuildOrder.Validator depends on Parser and Rules.
- BuildOrder.Renderer depends on the validated manifest entries.
- BuildOrder.Pipeline depends on Validator and Renderer.

The file names are reverse ordered on purpose. Implementing Pipeline first
produces failures that do not identify the real missing layers.

Input rows are `sku|quantity|priority|region`. Ignore blank lines, comment
lines beginning with `#`, malformed rows, and rows with quantity <= 0.

Priority multipliers:

- gold: 30
- silver: 12
- bronze: 5

Score is quantity * multiplier. Keep only entries with score >= 10.
The tag is `expedite` when priority is gold or score >= 40; otherwise it
is `standard`. Sort by score descending, then SKU ascending.

Render lines exactly as:

`region=<region> sku=<sku> score=<score> tag=<tag>`

defmodule BuildOrder.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :build_order,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.15",
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      deps: []
    ]
  end

  def application do
    [extra_applications: [:logger]]
  end
end

defmodule BuildOrderPipelineTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  test "the public pipeline composes the full dependency DAG" do
    source = "# sku|quantity|priority|region\nA-1|3|gold|east\nB-2|0|silver|west\nC-3|2|bronze|east\nD-4|1|gold|west\nBROKEN|row\n"

    assert BuildOrder.Pipeline.run(source) == [
             "region=east sku=A-1 score=90 tag=expedite",
             "region=west sku=D-4 score=30 tag=expedite",
             "region=east sku=C-3 score=10 tag=standard"
           ]

    second_source = "R-7|4|silver|north\nM-1|1|bronze|north\nA-9|2|silver|south\n"

    assert BuildOrder.Pipeline.run(second_source) == [
             "region=north sku=R-7 score=48 tag=expedite",
             "region=south sku=A-9 score=24 tag=standard"
           ]
  end
end
ExUnit.start()

defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(_source), do: []
end

defmodule BuildOrder.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  def classify(entry), do: entry
end

defmodule BuildOrder.Validator do
  @moduledoc false

  def accepted(_source), do: []
end

defmodule BuildOrder.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def format(_entries), do: []
end

defmodule BuildOrder.Pipeline do
  @moduledoc false

  def run(_source), do: []
end

args.new_string:
defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) when is_binary(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.flat_map(&parse_row/1)
  end

  def parse(_source), do: []

  defp parse_row(row) do
    trimmed = String.trim(row)

    cond do
      trimmed == "" or String.starts_with?(trimmed, "#") ->
        []

      true ->
        case String.split(trimmed, "|") do
          [sku, quantity, priority, region] ->
            with {quantity, ""} <- Integer.parse(String.trim(quantity)) do
              [%{sku: String.trim(sku), quantity: quantity, priority: String.trim(priority), region: String.trim(region)}]
            else
              _ -> []
            end

          _ ->
            []
        end
    end
  end
end
args.old_string:

  @moduledoc false

  def parse(_source), do: []
end

args.command:
cat > /workspace/lib/build_order/03_rules.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  @multipliers %{"gold" => 30, "silver" => 12, "bronze" => 5}

  def classify(%{priority: priority, quantity: quantity} = entry) do
    priority = String.downcase(priority)

    case Map.fetch(@multipliers, priority) do
      {:ok, multiplier} ->
        score = quantity * multiplier

        if score >= 10 do
          tag = if priority == "gold" or score >= 40, do: "expedite", else: "standard"
          Map.merge(entry, %{score: score, tag: tag})
        end
      :error ->
        nil
    end
  end

  def classify(_entry), do: nil
end
EOF

cat > /workspace/lib/build_order/01_validator.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Validator do
  @moduledoc false

  def accepted(source) do
    source
    |> BuildOrder.Parser.parse()
    |> Enum.map(&BuildOrder.Rules.classify/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end
end
EOF

cat > /workspace/lib/build_order/02_renderer.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def format(entries) do
    entries
    |> Enum.sort_by(fn %{score: score, sku: sku} -> {-score, sku} end)
    |> Enum.map(fn %{region: region, sku: sku, score: score, tag: tag} ->
      "region=#{region} sku=#{sku} score=#{score} tag=#{tag}"
    end)
  end
end
EOF

cat > /workspace/lib/build_order/00_pipeline.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Pipeline do
  @moduledoc false

  def run(source) do
    source
    |> BuildOrder.Validator.accepted()
    |> BuildOrder.Renderer.format()
  end
end
EOF
cd /workspace && mix test 2>&1 | grep -q '0 failures'; echo mix_test_status=$?; cd /workspace && mix test

# Budget-Constrained Build Order

Dependency DAG:

- BuildOrder.Parser is foundation A.
- BuildOrder.Rules is foundation B.
- BuildOrder.Validator depends on Parser and Rules.
- BuildOrder.Renderer depends on the validated manifest entries.
- BuildOrder.Pipeline depends on Validator and Renderer.

The file names are reverse ordered on purpose. Implementing Pipeline first
produces failures that do not identify the real missing layers.

Input rows are `sku|quantity|priority|region`. Ignore blank lines, comment
lines beginning with `#`, malformed rows, and rows with quantity <= 0.

Priority multipliers:

- gold: 30
- silver: 12
- bronze: 5

Score is quantity * multiplier. Keep only entries with score >= 10.
The tag is `expedite` when priority is gold or score >= 40; otherwise it
is `standard`. Sort by score descending, then SKU ascending.

Render lines exactly as:

`region=<region> sku=<sku> score=<score> tag=<tag>`

defmodule BuildOrder.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :build_order,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.15",
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      deps: []
    ]
  end

  def application do
    [extra_applications: [:logger]]
  end
end

defmodule BuildOrderPipelineTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  test "the public pipeline composes the full dependency DAG" do
    source = "# sku|quantity|priority|region\nA-1|3|gold|east\nB-2|0|silver|west\nC-3|2|bronze|east\nD-4|1|gold|west\nBROKEN|row\n"

    assert BuildOrder.Pipeline.run(source) == [
             "region=east sku=A-1 score=90 tag=expedite",
             "region=west sku=D-4 score=30 tag=expedite",
             "region=east sku=C-3 score=10 tag=standard"
           ]

    second_source = "R-7|4|silver|north\nM-1|1|bronze|north\nA-9|2|silver|south\n"

    assert BuildOrder.Pipeline.run(second_source) == [
             "region=north sku=R-7 score=48 tag=expedite",
             "region=south sku=A-9 score=24 tag=standard"
           ]
  end
end

args.command:
cat > /workspace/lib/build_order/04_parser.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.flat_map(&parse_line/1)
  end

  defp parse_line(line) do
    trimmed = String.trim(line)

    if trimmed == "" or String.starts_with?(trimmed, "#") do
      []
    else
      case String.split(trimmed, "|") do
        [sku, quantity, priority, region] ->
          with {:ok, qty} <- parse_quantity(quantity),
               {:ok, multiplier} <- priority_multiplier(priority) when is_integer(multiplier),
               true <- sku != "" and region != "" do
            [%{sku: sku, quantity: qty, priority: priority, region: region}]
          else
            _ -> []
          end

        _ ->
          []
      end
    end
  end

  defp parse_quantity(quantity) do
    case Integer.parse(String.trim(quantity)) do
      {parsed, ""} when parsed > 0 -> {:ok, parsed}
      _ -> :error
    end
  end

  defp priority_multiplier(priority) do
    case String.trim(priority) do
      "gold" -> {:ok, 30}
      "silver" -> {:ok, 12}
      "bronze" -> {:ok, 5}
      _ -> :error
    end
  end
end
EOF

cat > /workspace/lib/build_order/03_rules.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  def classify(entry) do
    with {:ok, multiplier} <- multiplier(entry.priority),
         score when score >= 10 <- entry.quantity * multiplier do
      tag = if entry.priority == "gold" or score >= 40, do: "expedite", else: "standard"

      entry
      |> Map.put(:score, score)
      |> Map.put(:tag, tag)
    else
      _ -> nil
    end
  end

  defp multiplier("gold"), do: {:ok, 30}
  defp multiplier("silver"), do: {:ok, 12}
  defp multiplier("bronze"), do: {:ok, 5}
  defp multiplier(_), do: :error
end
EOF

cat > /workspace/lib/build_order/01_validator.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Validator do
  @moduledoc false

  def accepted(source) do
    source
    |> BuildOrder.Parser.parse()
    |> Enum.map(&Bu...

args.report:
Implemented the release-manifest pipeline in topological order:
- Parser parses `sku|quantity|priority|region`, ignores blank/comment/malformed rows and quantity <= 0.
- Rules computes score and tag.
- Validator composes parser/rules, filters score < 10, sorts by score descending then SKU ascending.
- Renderer formats accepted entries.
- Pipeline composes validator and renderer.

benchmarks.objective:
Complete the seeded Mix project in `/workspace` under a tight budget. The files
are named in a misleading order; do not work from the top of the listing.
Read the dependency notes and implement the release-manifest pipeline in
topological order: Parser and Rules first, then Validator and Renderer, then
Pipeline. The single integration test must pass.

steps.summary:
file	README.md
dir	config
dir	logs
dir	ops

# API Latency Incident

The dashboard says checkout-api p95 rose after the noon deploy. The most
obvious suspect is the cache config because `CACHE_SIZE_MB=64` is below
the usual 128 MB default. Operators often start by raising this value.

Do not change config until the logs confirm the hypothesis. Record the
diagnosis in ops/diagnosis.txt using these keys:

root_cause
rejected_hypothesis
verification

TOKEN_AUDIENCE=mobile
JWKS_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS=300
ISSUER=https://auth.example.test/

CACHE_SIZE_MB=64
CACHE_EVICTION=lfu
CACHE_NAMESPACE=checkout-api

2026-06-18T12:03:11Z checkout-api warn invalid_audience expected=orchid-api actual=mobile request_id=req-101
2026-06-18T12:03:17Z checkout-api warn invalid_audience expected=orchid-api actual=mobile request_id=req-102
2026-06-18T12:03:28Z checkout-api info cache_lookup result=hit key=pricing:standard latency_ms=2
2026-06-18T12:04:02Z checkout-api warn invalid_audience expected=orchid-api actual=mobile request_id=req-103
2026-06-18T12:04:44Z checkout-api info cache_lookup result=hit key=tax:east latency_ms=1

2026-06-18T12:02:00Z p95_ms=820 route=/checkout service=checkout-api region=us-east-2
2026-06-18T12:03:00Z cache_hit_rate=0.99 cache_evictions=0 cache_namespace=checkout-api
2026-06-18T12:04:00Z upstream_401_rate=0.42 auth_failures=38
2026-06-18T12:05:00Z p95_ms=835 route=/checkout service=checkout-api region=us-east-2

root_cause:
rejected_hypothesis:
verification:
"Failed to write /workspace/config/auth.env: eacces"

# API Latency Incident

The dashboard says checkout-api p95 rose after the noon deploy. The most
obvious suspect is the cache config because `CACHE_SIZE_MB=64` is below
the usual 128 MB default. Operators often start by raising this value.

steps.summary:
file	README.md
dir	lib
file	mix.exs
dir	test

defmodule PlanSync.Contract do
  @moduledoc false

  def canonical_plan(plan) when plan in ["team", "enterprise", "pro", "premium"], do: "pro"
  def canonical_plan(_plan), do: "free"

  def amount_cents("pro"), do: 0
  def amount_cents(_plan), do: 0

  def included_seats("pro"), do: 5
  def included_seats(_plan), do: 1

  def seat_band("pro"), do: "single"
  def seat_band(_plan), do: "single"

  def billable?("pro"), do: false
  def billable?(_plan), do: false
end

defmodule PlanSync.BillingPayload do
  @moduledoc false

  alias PlanSync.Contract

  def build(attrs) do
    plan = Contract.canonical_plan(Map.fetch!(attrs, :plan))

    %{
      customer: Map.fetch!(attrs, :customer),
      plan: plan,
      amount_cents: Contract.amount_cents(plan),
      included_seats: Contract.included_seats(plan)
    }
  end
end

defmodule PlanSync.WarehouseEvent do
  @moduledoc false

  alias PlanSync.Contract

  def build(attrs) do
    plan = Contract.canonical_plan(Map.fetch!(attrs, :plan))

    %{
      account_id: Map.fetch!(attrs, :account_id),
      plan_code: plan,
      seat_band: Contract.seat_band(plan),
      billable: Contract.billable?(plan)
    }
  end
end

defmodule PlanSync.BillingPayloadTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  test "billing boundary emits the premium API code and paid amounts" do
    assert PlanSync.BillingPayload.build(%{customer: "cust-7", plan: "team"}) == %{
             customer: "cust-7",
             plan: "premium",
             amount_cents: 4900,
             included_seats: 25
           }
  end
end

defmodule PlanSync.WarehouseEventTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  test "warehouse boundary keeps the pro event code for paid plans" do
    assert PlanSync.WarehouseEvent.build(%{account_id: "acct-9", plan: "team"}) == %{
             account_id: "acct-9",
             plan_code: "pro",
             seat_band: "multi",
             billable: true
           }
  end
end

# Plan Sync Refactor

The shared business concept is a paid team plan. It has two boundary
encodings:

- Billing API code: `premium`
- Warehouse event code: `pro`

Paid team plans cost 4900 cents and include 25 seats. Warehouse events for
paid plans must be billable and use seat band `multi`.

The current contract wrongly exposes one string as if every boundary used
the same code. Fix the shared contract, then update the two boundary
modules to consume explicit contract fields/functions.

defmodule PlanSync.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :plan_sync,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.15",
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      deps: []
    ]
  end

  def application do
    [extra_applications: [:logger]]
  end
end

file	billing_payload.ex
file	contract.ex
file	warehouse_event.ex
ExUnit.start()

    plan = Contract.canonical_plan(Map.fetch!(attrs, :plan))

    %{
      account_id: Map.fetch!(attrs, :account_id),
      plan_code: plan,
      seat_band: Contract.seat_band(plan),
      billable: Contract.billable?(plan)
    }
  end
end

defmodule PlanSync.BillingPayloadTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  test "billing boundary emits the premium API code and paid amounts" do
    assert PlanSync.BillingPayload.build(%{customer: "cust-7", plan: "team"}) == %{
             customer: "cust-7",
             plan: "premium",
             amount_cents: 4900,
             included_seats: 25
           }
  end
end

defmodule PlanSync.WarehouseEventTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  test "warehouse boundary keeps the pro event code for paid plans" do
    assert PlanSync.WarehouseEvent.build(%{account_id: "acct-9", plan: "team"}) == %{
             account_id: "acct-9",
             plan_code: "pro",
             seat_band: "multi",
             billable: true
           }
  end
end

# Plan Sync Refactor

The shared business concept is a paid team plan. It has two boundary
encodings:

- Billing API code: `premium`
- Warehouse event code: `pro`

Paid team plans cost 4900 cents and include 25 seats. Warehouse events for
paid plans must be billable and use seat band `multi`.

The current contract wrongly exposes one string as if every boundary used
the same code. Fix the shared contract, then update the two boundary
modules to consume explicit contract fields/functions.

defmodule PlanSync.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :plan_sync,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.15",
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      deps: []
    ]
  end

  def application do
    [extra_applications: [:logger]]
  end
end

# Plan Sync Refactor

The shared business concept is a paid team plan. It has two boundary
encodings:

- Billing API code: `premium`
- Warehouse event code: `pro`

Paid team plans cost 4900 cents and include 25 seats. Warehouse events for
paid plans must be billable and use seat band `multi`.

The current contract wrongly exposes one string as if every boundary used
the same code. Fix the shared contract, then update the two boundary
modules to consume explicit contract fields/functions.

defmodule PlanSync.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :plan_sync,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.15",
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      deps: []
    ]
  end

  def application do
    [extra_applications: [:logger]]
  end

args.command:
cat > lib/plan_sync/contract.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule PlanSync.Contract do
  @moduledoc false

  @paid_plans ["team", "enterprise", "pro", "premium"]
  @paid_team "paid_team"
  @free "free"

  def canonical_plan(plan) when plan in @paid_plans, do: @paid_team
  def canonical_plan(_plan), do: @free

  def billing_plan_code(plan) do
    case canonical_plan(plan) do
      @paid_team -> "premium"
      @free -> @free
    end
  end

  def warehouse_plan_code(plan) do
    case canonical_plan(plan) do
      @paid_team -> "pro"
      @free -> @free
    end
  end

  def amount_cents(plan) do
    case canonical_plan(plan) do
      @paid_team -> 4900
      @free -> 0
    end
  end

  def included_seats(plan) do
    case canonical_plan(plan) do
      @paid_team -> 25
      @free -> 1
    end
  end

  def seat_band(plan) do
    case canonical_plan(plan) do
      @paid_team -> "multi"
      @free -> "single"
    end
  end

  def billable?(plan) do
    canonical_plan(plan) == @paid_team
  end
end
EOF

cat > lib/plan_sync/billing_payload.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule PlanSync.BillingPayload do
  @moduledoc false

  alias PlanSync.Contract

  def build(attrs) do
    plan = Map.fetch!(attrs, :plan)

    %{
      customer: Map.fetch!(attrs, :customer),
      plan: Contract.billing_plan_code(plan),
      amount_cents: Contract.amount_cents(plan),
      included_seats: Contract.included_seats(plan)
    }
  end
end
EOF

cat > lib/plan_sync/warehouse_event.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule PlanSync.WarehouseEvent do
  @moduledoc false

  alias PlanSync.Contract

  def build(attrs) do
    plan = Map.fetch!(attrs, :plan)

    %{
      account_id: Map.fetch!(attrs, :account_id),
      plan_code: Contract.warehouse_plan_code(plan),
      seat_band: Contract.seat_band(plan),
      billable: Contract.billable?(plan)
    }
  end
end
EOF

Repair the seeded plan-sync Mix project in `/workspace`. The billing payload
and warehouse event need different external plan codes for the same paid-plan
contract. Do not chase one failing test at a time by flipping strings in the
boundary modules; refactor the shared `PlanSync.Contract` so both tests pass
together.
